question.text:
For a statement S in a program, in the context of 
liveness analysis, the following sets are defined 

USE(S) : the set of variables used in S 

IN (S) : the set of variables that are live at the entry 
of S  

OUT (S) : the set of variables that are live at the exit 
of S 

Consider a basic block that consists of two 
statements, S1 followed by S2.  

Which one of the following statements is correct?

solution.text:
For two statements S1 and S2, If S2 executes after 
S1 then we will first compute IN and OUT sets for 
S2 and then we will do it for S1. Here OUT(S1) will 
be same as IN(S2) in the backward analysis. 


OUT (S1) purely depends on IN(S2).  

Therefor, OUT(S1) = IN(S2). 

Option A is the correct answer.

questions.subtopic: Which one of the following statements is correct?
tags: Which one of the following statements is correct?

question.text:
Which of the following statements are TRUE? 

I: 
There exist parsing algorithms for some 
programming languages whose complexities 
are 
less than θ(n3). 

II: A programming language which allows 
recursion can be implemented with static storage 
allocation. 

III: No L-attributed definition can be evaluated in 
the framework of bottom-up  parsing. 

IV: Code improving transformations can be 
performed at both source language and intermediate 
code level.

solution.text:
I. 
"There exist parsing algorithms for some 
programming languages whose complexities 
are less than θ(n3)" is TRUE. For example, 
CYK algorithm.  
II. 
"A programming language which allows 
recursion can be implemented with static 

storage allocation" is FALSE. A programming 
language which allows recursion can be 
implemented with stack storage allocation. 
III. "No L-attributed definition can be evaluated in 
the framework of bottom-up parsing" is 
FALSE. Every S-attributed definition is also 
L-attributed definition and hence they can be 
evaluated in the framework of bottom-up 
parsing.  
IV. "Code improving transformations can be 
performed at both source language and 
intermediate code level" is TRUE.  

So, statement I and IV are true, and hence option b 
is the correct amswer.

question.text:
Consider the basic block given below. 

 a = b + c  

 c = a + d 

 d = b + c 

 e = d − b 

 a = e + b 

The minimum number of nodes and edges present in 
the DAG representation of the above basic block 
respectively are

solution.text:
a = e +b 

= (d - b) + b 

= ([b + c] - b) + b  
= ([b + (a + d) ] - b) + b 

= ([b + (b + c] + d) - b) + b 

a = b + ([b + c] + d) 

Corresponding DAG representation: 

There are total 6 nodes and 6 edges. So, option a is 
correct answer.

question.text:
GATE-2014 : 1M] 

Which one of the following is FALSE?

options.text:
A basic block is a sequence of instructions 
where control enters the sequence at the 
beginning and exits at the end.

Available expression analysis can be used for 
common subexpression elimination.

Live variable analysis can be used for dead 
code elimination.

x = 4 \times 5 ⇒ x = 20 is an example of common 
subexpression elimination.

solution.text:
(a) "A basic block is a sequence of instructions 
where control enters the sequence at the 
beginning and exits at the end" is TRUE. In a 
basic block control only enters at the beginning 
and exits at the end. Control never enters in the 
middle of the instruction and never exits from 
the middle of the sequence of instructions.  

(b) "Available expression analysis can be used for 
common subexpression elimination" is TRUE 

(c) "Live variable analysis can be used for dead 
code elimination" is TRUE, with backward 
analysis we can use live variable for dead code 
elimination.  

(d) "x = 4 \times 5 ⇒ x = 20 is an example of common 
subexpression elimination" is FALSE. This is 
an example of constant folding.  

So, option D is correct answer.

Given, P(X) = X5 + 4X3 + 6X + 5 




= X5 + 4X3 + 6X + 5 




=   X [X4 + 4X2 + 6] + 5  


So, there are total 4 multiplication and 3 additions, 
so total 7 arithmetic operation are required.

question.text:
Suppose the instruction set architecture of the 
processor has only two registers. The only allowed 
compiler optimization is code motion, which moves 
statements from one place to another while 
preserving correctness. What is the minimum 
number of spills to memory in the compiled code?

solution.text:
Sol. Spill to memory means how many extra 
registers/memory/variables are required.  

Code motion can be performed as follows: 

c = a + b; //a and b in register and b replaced by the 
result c after the instruction 

x = c * c; //x replaces c in register and c is spilled 
(moved to memory) 

if (x > a) { //x and a in memory 

y = a * a;  

 d = c * a; //spilled c taken from memory and 
replaces x in register.  

e = c + a;  

}  

else {  

d = c * a; //spilled c taken from memory and replaces 
x in register. d replaces a in register 

d = d * d; //c and d are in register 

e = c + a; //a  is taken from memory and e replaces 
c in the register (a taken from memory is not a spill, 
it is a fill) 

e = e * e;  

}  

So, minimum 1 spill is required in the compiled code.

question.text:
What is the minimum number of registers needed in 
the instruction set architecture of the processor to 
compile this code segment without any spill to 
memory? Do not apply any optimization other than 
optimizing register allocation.

Consider evaluating the following expression tree on 
a machine with load-store architecture in which 
memory can be accessed only through load and store 
instructions. The variables a, b, c, d and e are initially 
stored in memory. The binary operators used in this 
expression tree can be evaluated by the machine only 
when the operands are in registers. The instructions 
produce result only in a register. If no intermediate 
results can be stored in memory, what is the 
minimum number of registers needed to evaluate this 
expression?

solution.text:
From the above diagram we can see, three registers 
are required R1, R2, and R3. Therefore, option d is 
the correct answer.

intermediate code because they enhance the 
portability of the compiler to the target processors.

question.text:
Consider the following grammar: 

Stmt \rightarrow if expr then expr else expr; stmt |ε 

expr \rightarrow term relop term | term 

term \rightarrow id | number 

id \rightarrow a | b | c 

number→[0-9] 

where relop is a relation operator (e.g., <,>,…), 0 
refers to the empty statement, and if, then, else are 
terminals. 

Consider a program P following the above grammar 
containing ten if terminals. The number of control 
flow paths in P is ______. if 𝑒1 then 𝑒2 else 𝑒3 

has 2 control flow paths, e1→e2 and e1→e3.

solution.text:
if e1 then e2  

else e3 


2 \times 2 \times ….. 10 times = 210 = 1024 paths

question.text:
In the context of abstract-syntax-tree (AST) and 
control-flow-graph (CFG), which one of the 
following is True?

options.text:
In both AST and CFG, let node N2 be the 
successor of node N1. In the input program, 
the code corresponding to N2 is present after 
the code corresponding to N1

For any input program, neither AST nor CFG 
will contain a cycle.

The maximum number of successors of a node 
in an AST and a CFG depends on the input 
program.

Each node in AST and CFG corresponds to at 
most one statement in the input program.

solution.text:
(a) "In both AST and CFG, let node N2 be the 
successor of node N1. In the input program, the 
code corresponding to N2 is present after the 
code corresponding to N1" is FALSE. If loop is 
present than control can move from any 
statement to any statement.  
(b) "For any input program, neither AST nor CFG 
will contain a cycle" is FALSE. CFG will have 
a cycle. 
(c) "The maximum number of successors of a 
node in an AST and a CFG depends on the 
input program" is TRUE.   
(d) "Each node in AST and CFG corresponds to at 
most one statement in the input program" is 
FALSE.  
Only option (c) is correct.

question.text:
Match all items in Group 1 with correct options 
from those given in Group 2. 


 Group 1 

Group 2 

P:  Regular expression 
1.  Syntax analysis 

Q:  Pushdown automata 2.  Code generation 

R:  Dataflow analysis 
3.  Lexical analysis 

S: Register allocation

solution.text:
Regular expression is used in lexical analysis phase.  

Pushdown automata is used in syntax analysis 
phase. 

Dataflow analysis is a part of code optimisation. 

Register allocation is used in code generation while 
generating assembly code.  

So, option (b) is the correct answer.
